title: Endpoints should not be vulnerable to reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks
description: Reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) occurs in a web application when the application retrieves data like parameters or headers from an incoming HTTP request and inserts it into its HTTP response without first sanitizing it. The most common cause is the insertion of GET parameters. When well-intentioned users open a link to a page that is vulnerable to reflected XSS, they are exposed to attacks that target their own browser.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Controller methods that return HTML content with unencoded user input parameters
logic.triggering_conditions: (method returns HTML/Content) AND (uses request parameter in output) AND (no HTML encoding)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes JSON responses, encoded parameters, non-HTML content types
logic.never_trigger_on: JSON responses, HtmlEncoder usage, HttpUtility.HtmlEncode, Razor views with automatic encoding
logic.reasoning: Reflected XSS occurs when user input is inserted into HTML without encoding. Detects patterns: Return Content with HTML content type + parameter interpolation without HtmlEncode/HtmlEncoder. Also detects ViewBag assignments without encoding. This is simplified check focusing on obvious cases.

checks.message: Encode user input before inserting into HTML to prevent XSS
Encode user input before inserting into HTML to prevent XSS
Encode user input before assigning to ViewBag
